chapter_title.ar: باب تَرْبِيعِ الْقَبْرِ وَ رَشِّهِ بِالْمَاءِ وَ مَا يُقَالُ عِنْدَ ذَلِكَ وَ قَدْرِ مَا يُرْفَعُ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 67 – Sqaring of the grave, and sprinkling it with the water, and what is to be said during that, and the measurement of what it can be raised from the ground

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: فيه إرسال - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/112)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: فيه إرسال - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/112)
text: قَامَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَلَى قَبْرِ رَجُلٍ مِنَ الشِّيعَةِ فَقَالَ اللَّهُمَّ صِلْ وَحْدَتَهُ وَ آنِسْ وَحْشَتَهُ وَ أَسْكِنْ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ رَحْمَتِكَ مَا يَسْتَغْنِي بِهَا عَنْ رَحْمَةِ مَنْ سِوَاكَ .

en.hubeali: Humeyd bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Muhammad, from someone else, from Aban, from Abdullah Bin Ajlan who said,

‘Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> stood upon the grave of a man from the Shiah and he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! Grant companionship in his loneliness, and Comfort his fear, and Settle to him from Your<sup>azwj</sup> Mercy what would make him to be needless from a mercy from anyone besides You<sup>azwj</sup>’’.
en.sarwar: 9. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Muhammad from more than one person from Aban from ‘Abd Allah ibn ‘Ajalan who has said the following: “Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, stood on the grave of one of his followers and said, ‘O Lord, grant company in his loneliness, comfort his fear and settle down with him of your mercy that much which will make him free from want from others except yourself.’”
